--------------------
Analyzing call stack context and environment
--------------------
Replaced the Sina scraper’s GBK decoding with the platform-provided 
--------------------
TextDecoder
--------------------
, removing the 
--------------------
Buffer
--------------------
/
--------------------
iconv-lite
--------------------
 dependency so the crawl API can execute cleanly in edge runtimes that lack 
--------------------
 (
--------------------
sina.ts (line 19)
--------------------
). The decoder now normalizes charset hints, falls back to UTF‑8, and logs once when an unexpected encoding appears.
--------------------
Tests were not run because the local Node runtime isn’t available in this environment.
--------------------
Suggested next step: 1) Restart or rebuild the Next dev server so the API route picks up the new decoding logic.
--------------------
重新用中文解释一下
--------------------
Preparing explanation in Chinese
--------------------
刚刚的修改主要是把新浪抓取器里网页内容的解码方式从 
--------------------
 + 
--------------------
 改成原生的 
--------------------
，这样在 Node.js 中抓取 GBK/UTF-8 编码的文章时就不用依赖 
--------------------
。因为 Next.js 的 edge/runtime 环境里没有 
--------------------
，原先的实现会直接报错，现在通过 
--------------------
 处理后，就能在这些环境里正常运行。如果遇到不认识的编码，也会先打个警告并自动回退到 UTF-8。
--------------------
✓ Compiled /api/crawl in 315ms (883 modules)
Database schema ensured successfully

--------------------
请分析
--------------------
Analyzing ReferenceError for File global
--------------------
已将新浪抓取器的解码逻辑改为显式从 
--------------------
node:util
--------------------
 引入 
--------------------
（
--------------------
sina.ts (line 2)
--------------------
），避免 Next 的运行时在加载全局 
--------------------
 时触发到依赖 
--------------------
File
--------------------
 的浏览器 polyfill，从而导致 “ReferenceError: File is not defined”。现在仍然依据响应头判断 
--------------------
gb18030/gbk/utf-8
--------------------
，遇到不支持编码会记录警告并回退到 UTF-8。
--------------------
未执行自动化测试——建议重新启动/构建 dev 服务器验证 
--------------------
/api/crawl
--------------------
 是否恢复正常。
--------------------
要求后续变更
--------------------
自动提供背景信息
--------------------
